danger_signals.visual_form: Alien sprite formations moving across screen from right edge
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion and counterattack

danger_signals.visual_form: Blue-colored sphere(s) centered in large enemy craft body
danger_signals.meaning: Primary danger and critical weak point; core changes from blue to red when critically damaged

danger_signals.visual_form: Small blocking structures positioned between player fire and boss core
danger_signals.meaning: Impediment to victory; must be worn down through sustained fire before core becomes vulnerable

danger_signals.signal: Slow player movement at game start
danger_signals.visual_form: Vic Viper sprite with limited firing range and speed
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerable state; insufficient capability for most combat engagements

safety_signals.visual_form: Capsule items appearing on screen after enemy defeat
safety_signals.meaning: Advancement toward greater capability; player selects from power-up menu to gain weapons and attributes

safety_signals.visual_form: Power-up menu at bottom of screen with highlighted desired upgrade
safety_signals.meaning: Player has secured capability increase when button pressed; menu resets after power-up activation

safety_signals.visual_form: Boss core changes from blue to red visual state
safety_signals.meaning: Boss entering critical condition; destruction is imminent after sustained damage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Single distinctive player-controlled vessel contrasted against multiple alien enemy types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player positioned on left side of horizontally-scrolling screen; occupies lower screen area during gameplay

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Varied alien creature designs across stages (including Moai statue enemies in stage 3)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple distinct enemy types with unique sprite designs per stage; boss crafts feature blue core spheres as faction identifier
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Emerge from right screen edge; occupy various environmental niches including organic and mechanical terrain

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vic Viper displayed with minimal sprite size; limited weapon fire pattern visible on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player moves slowly; weak single-shot or narrow firing pattern; vulnerable to basic enemy contact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Capsule item appears after enemy defeat; power-up menu illuminates at screen bottom with new selection highlighted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vic Viper gains additional weapon types, speed enhancement, shield capability (Options), or improved firing rate depending on menu selection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large alien craft fills screen; multiple blue core spheres visible within craft body; destructible wall barriers positioned between craft exterior and cores
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss performs weapon attack patterns from body emplacements; player must navigate projectiles while targeting specific passage leading to core; walls regenerate on some bosses

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss core changes color from blue to red; craft body may show damage effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss nearing destruction; one final core hit typically results in craft explosion and stage completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Horizontally-scrolling backgrounds showing distinct visual themes per stage (organic, mechanical, monument-based); environmental features change with each stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy types and patterns shift with environment; player encounters stage-specific threats and visual obstacles

environmental_storytelling.technique: Stage-specific visual themes and enemy types
environmental_storytelling.example: Stage 3 features Moai statue enemies, establishing a monument/organic environment distinct from mechanical stages; each stage has unique background scrolling and enemy configurations that communicate world progression

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss craft design as faction architecture
environmental_storytelling.example: Blue-cored boss crafts establish alien mechanical aesthetic; craft design and weapon placement communicate threat level and combat structure

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Menu position at screen bottom provides persistent visual reference to player upgrade state; advancement through menu options shows progression toward greater combat capability

environmental_storytelling.example: Small walls between player fire and boss core represent environmental impediment that must be systematically destroyed, creating visual evidence of progress toward core vulnerability

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Slow movement speed; weak single-fire gun; vulnerable collision state
Increased movement speed; enhanced weapon fire (spread shots, rapid fire, or doubled output); option satellites added for enhanced coverage
Fires projectiles from weapon emplacements; maintains position; wall barriers remain intact between craft exterior and core
Core color remains blue; craft continues attack patterns; walls may regenerate if damaged
Core color changes to red; one additional hit triggers destruction sequence and boss explosion
Approach from right screen edge in formation; attack player position; defeated enemies drop power-up capsules

notes: Gradius (1986 NES) uses a horizontally-scrolling shmup paradigm with world-state communication primarily through: (1) power-meter menu system that visually tracks capability progression, (2) boss core color-coding (blue=vulnerable state progression, red=critical), (3) stage-specific visual theming that establishes environmental context, (4) contrasting player/enemy sprite design that clearly delineates faction boundaries. The game lacks explicit political or narrative faction systems; instead, communication is fundamentally mechanical: visual signals indicate game-state through sprite condition, menu state, and environmental presence. Core danger signals are color-coded (blue cores) and positioned as clear weak points. Safety progression is tied to power-up acquisition and menu advancement. The two-fires manifestation pattern is implicit rather than explicit—world-state is communicated through gameplay mechanics (movement speed, fire rate, core color) rather than diegetic narrative elements.
